headline: myappai turns natural-language requests into pages, workflows, payments, and deploy-ready execution
subheadline: Run one cleaner command center for the public funnel, implementation intake, deployment activity, and operator-grade administration. The redesign is built to feel more premium, move faster, and close with less friction.

heroPanel.heading: One command center, three clear revenue lanes.
heroPanel.body: Software buyers can check out fast, implementation buyers can submit a serious brief, and enterprise buyers can qualify rollout without getting pushed through the wrong flow.

points.label: Operator OS
points.value: Direct software lane for teams ready to move now.

points.label: Launch Sprint
points.value: Guided implementation lane for teams that want setup help.

points.label: Enterprise Deployment
points.value: Qualified rollout lane for governed environments and custom scope.

workflowHeadline: The platform is designed to move from prompt to approved action without making the product feel messy underneath.

workflowSteps.title: Capture the request
workflowSteps.description: A buyer or operator explains what needs to change in plain language.

workflowSteps.title: Route the right lane
workflowSteps.description: The system decides whether the next move is content, implementation, payment, or deployment.

workflowSteps.title: Ship with visibility
workflowSteps.description: Leads, offers, command activity, and release state stay visible instead of disappearing behind hidden operations.

buyerPathsIntro: Every serious visitor should be able to see the correct next step in seconds.

buyerPaths.title: Buy Operator OS
buyerPaths.description: The core software offer for teams that already understand the value and want direct access now.
buyerPaths.outcome: Fastest path from conviction to recurring revenue.

buyerPaths.title: Start Launch Sprint
buyerPaths.description: A guided setup lane for teams that want the first system built with them instead of handed over cold.
buyerPaths.outcome: Best path for service revenue and faster activation.

buyerPaths.title: Scope Enterprise Deployment
buyerPaths.description: A review-driven lane for organizations with governance, rollout, hosting, or compliance requirements.
buyerPaths.outcome: Best path for larger contracts and deeper integration.

audienceIntro: The new public funnel is intentionally designed for the buyers most likely to value structure, speed, and deployable execution.

audiences.title: Founders and operators
audiences.description: Use one control system to turn new offers and ideas into live pages and shippable workflow changes.
bullets: Reduce the mess between idea and execution
Keep offers and delivery paths coherent
Launch without bolting together five tools

audiences.title: Service teams
audiences.description: Package implementation and delivery work into a tighter premium workflow with better handoff.
bullets: Turn setup work into a repeatable sprint
Use the same stack for sales and delivery

audiences.title: Enterprise stakeholders
audiences.description: Keep the higher-trust lane ready for deployment planning, governance review, and custom rollout work.
bullets: Route larger deals into qualification first

proofHeadline: Trust comes from visible operations, not decorative claims.
whyNowHeadline: Why this system closes faster than the old sprawl

whyNowCards.title: The UI is cleaner and more decisive
whyNowCards.description: The redesign removes dead-end pages and surfaces the next action more aggressively.

whyNowCards.title: The admin system uses the same product truth as the public funnel
whyNowCards.description: Offers, deploys, analytics, and content changes all stay connected.

whyNowCards.title: The operator can use natural language or direct commands
whyNowCards.description: Custom GPT mode and structured JSON commands now target the real app.

founderStory.heading: This version is built around what the system can actually do right now.
founderStory.body: The strongest trust layer comes from observable capability: checkout readiness, lead routing, deployment visibility, and the admin console operating against the live app instead of a dead legacy shell.
points: Custom GPT and admin command flow

productsIntro: The offer structure is clearer, more premium, and easier to navigate.
objectionHeadline: Questions the redesign answers before buyers leave

objections.question: Is this just another generic AI wrapper?
objections.answer: No. The product is positioned as an AI system manager with deployment, payment routing, and operator control built into the same stack.

objections.question: What if I need hands-on help before I buy software?
objections.answer: That is what Launch Sprint is for. The guided lane now has a stronger path from brief to execution.

objections.question: What if I need a more governed rollout?
objections.answer: Enterprise Deployment stays qualification-first so rollout decisions are grounded in reality.

faq.question: Does the custom GPT actually edit the live app now?
faq.answer: Yes. The operator console now points at the live frontend and content layers instead of the old legacy roots.

faq.question: Can PayPal still work without the full PayPal API setup?
faq.answer: Yes. PayPal can route through mr.jwswain@gmail.com as a fallback when direct API credentials are not configured.

faq.question: How does deployment happen?
faq.answer: Deployment flows through git-based release operations with visibility in the admin surface and a safer changed-path deploy handoff.

finalCta.heading: Go straight to pricing if you are ready to buy, or submit a brief if you want a guided rollout.
finalCta.body: The redesign is intentionally sharper about the next action so serious buyers and operators reach the right flow faster.
